channel.title: math.HO updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.HO updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Analogues of a formula of Ferrar: what I have learned from Semyon Yakubovich
item.description: arXiv:2604.09707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: W. L. Ferrar seems to have been the first mathematician to clearly draw a connection between the functional aspects of a summation formula and the behavior of the Dirichlet series underlying it. Taking a formula due to him as a starting point, I will describe some new generalizations of Ferrar's formulas and how these were actually obtained after learning a great deal from Semyon. I also present a very concise overview of the underlying theory of summation formulas and how the Mellin transform has been the link between mine and Professor Yakubovich's interests.
item.dc:creator: Pedro Ribeiro

item.title: A Minimal Mathematical Model for Conducting Patterns
item.description: arXiv:2604.10356v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a minimal mathematical model for conducting patterns that separates geometric trajectory from temporal parametrization. The model is based on a cyclic sequence of preparation and ictus points connected by cubic Hermite segments with constrained horizontal tangents, combined with a quintic timing law controlling acceleration and deceleration. A single parameter governs the balance between uniform motion and expressive emphasis. The model provides a compact yet expressive representation of conducting gestures. It is implemented as the interactive Wolfram Demonstration "Conducting Patterns" and is used in the Crusis web app.
item.dc:creator: Tom Verhoeff

item.title: Astrolabe: A Content-Addressable Hypergraph for Semantic Knowledge Management
item.description: arXiv:2604.10435v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing knowledge management tools either preserve prose but lose structural relationships, or capture relationships but restrict edge semantics to fixed vocabularies. We introduce Astrolabe, a content-addressable hypergraph for semantic knowledge management. Entries are identified by the SHA-256 hash of their content, carry an ordered reference list of arbitrary width, and store an opaque record string interpreted by plugins. The structure admits two orthogonal decompositions: by width and by depth. We demonstrate the framework with a plugin bridging informal and formal mathematics.
item.dc:creator: Xinze Li
